text.title: Post Launch Conversion Drip Core Rules
text.summary: 5+ testable rules every Post Launch Conversion Drip application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Tag every launch-week signup with source=launch-<channel>-<date> at capture time; without the tag the drip cannot be isolated from steady-state onboarding and you cannot measure its lift.
rule.rationale: Carries forward from v1 source; preserve until revised in next pass.

rule.statement: Send T+0 (within 2 hours of signup) with one job: confirm + set the next-step expectation; include the single quickstart link, no feature dump; >1 link reduces click-through by ~30% on this email per typical SaaS data.
rule.rationale: Carries forward from v1 source; preserve until revised in next pass.

rule.statement: Send T+2 (≈48h) with one job: deliver an aha-moment — a 60-second video, a sample report, or a templated "try this with your data" prompt; this is the highest-lift email of the drip, do not skip it.
rule.rationale: Carries forward from v1 source; preserve until revised in next pass.

rule.statement: Send T+7 with one job: social proof — one named case study with concrete numbers and a quote from a real user; generic testimonials reduce reply rate vs a named user with a logo.
rule.rationale: Carries forward from v1 source; preserve until revised in next pass.

rule.statement: Send T+14 with one job: ask for a decision — book a call, start trial, or one-question reply ("what's stopping you?"); the open-ended reply email out-performs hard CTAs for unactivated signups on this drip stage.
rule.rationale: Carries forward from v1 source; preserve until revised in next pass.

rule.statement: Suppress the drip if the user has already converted to paid or completed activation; running the drip on already-activated users dilutes engagement signal and risks unsubscribes.
rule.rationale: Carries forward from v1 source; preserve until revised in next pass.

rule.statement: Measure four KPIs per send (open, click, primary CTA conversion, unsubscribe) and one funnel KPI (signup → activated → paid by T+30 for this cohort); compare to your non-launch baseline cohort — if the drip does not beat baseline by ≥1.5× on conversion, rewrite, do not just retry.
rule.rationale: Carries forward from v1 source; preserve until revised in next pass.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
